title: goodenia macmillanii
intro: Goodenia macmillanii, also known as Macmillan's goodenia, is a flowering plant native to Australia. It has been traditionally used in Australian Indigenous medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of colds, fever, and sore throats. The plant's leaves and stems are said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of Goodenia macmillanii, such as teas and infusions, are often used to support immune function and provide relief from cold-related discomforts.
